I was promised destruction.

$QQQ
$AMZN Zoox has begun mapping streets in Dallas and Phoenix ahead of autonomous vehicle testing in both cities.

$MSFT introduced Copilot Cowork which is a new Microsoft 365 feature that turns user requests into plans and executes tasks across apps and files.

Another example of Microsoft packaging agentic AI into tools hundreds of millions of workers already use.

It’s been over a decade since $MSFT broke its 200-week EMA which now aligns with the anchored VWAP from the Oct 2022 bear-market bottom around the $385–$390 range.

Big level the stock needs to defend.
Congrats to the Airwallex team on officially crossing $1B in ARR  👏

Tom Lee's $BMNR now holds ~3.8% of all Ethereum.

They added ~61K $ETH last week bringing their total to ~4.53M.

$NVO confirms Wegovy and Ozempic will be sold on the $HIMS platform with Wegovy shots and the pill launching later this month.

Hims & Hers will also stop advertising compounded GLP-1 alternatives.

$ONDS PREANNOUNCES RESULTS

• Q4 Revenue: $30M vs. Est. $28M
• FY26 Guidance: $175M vs. Est. $174M

$AMZN Pharmacy now offers $LLY Zepbound KwikPen with cash-pay pricing starting at $299/month for the 2.5mg starter dose.

Amazon also plans to expand same-day pharmacy delivery from ~3,000 cities today to about 4,500 by the end of 2026.

$AAOI secured its first volume order for 1.6T data center transceivers from a major hyperscaler worth over $200M. 

Shipments are expected to begin in Q3’26 following product qualification and ramp through Q4.
$HIMS jumps ~48% after $NVO plans to sell its obesity drugs through the Hims platform.

G7 officials will discuss a coordinated emergency oil release Monday with some proposing a 300–400M barrel release through the IEA.

Oil prices pulled back from $120 to around $102 after reports that several G7 countries (including the U.S.) support the move.

$ORCL says reports about the Texas data center are “false and incorrect” noting two buildings are already operational and the rest of the campus remains on schedule.

The company also said it has secured an additional 4.5GW of capacity to support its OpenAI commitments.

5/5

The charts and table above show the market is pricing a short disruption of a few months in oil and not a significant elevation that “persists for the year.”

The main reason for this thinking is that there has been no real reports of long-term infrastructure damage. Tankers are not transiting the Strait of Hormuz, so storages in the Middle East are full. Production is slowing as a result. When the ships start moving again, the system will start supplying the world with crude.

3/5

Below is the calendar spread between the first contract (now April) and the 6th contract (now September).

As the bottom panel shows, this spread is -25%, a record since the mid-1990s when the contract specifications were last changed.

2/5

The SPX has corrected 5% for the first time since November.

Translation ... no TACO

*BRENT CRUDE OIL EXTENDS SURGE TO 25% ON MIDEAST WAR DISRUPTION

In addition to rallying 25% today (Sunday night), May Brent Crude oil futures are up 56% since last Wednesday's low (March 4th).

The national average of gasoline prices is up 45 cents since the war began last week, or a 15.12% increase.

This is the second largest increase in the last two decades, depending on the measure you choose (dollar or percentage).

All things equal, a 15% rise in gasoline prices adds about 0.45% to March headline CPI.

$NVDA $MSFT $AMZN $GOOG $META
$spy did a bit of market discovery with a few ways to make tactical cash flow.

📺 THE MARKET MAY BE SETTING UP A SOFTWARE REBOUND

The software sector is starting to look like a potential tactical trading opportunity, as the group may be stabilizing after a prolonged selloff, as buyers are stepping in.

The key question is whether software $IGV can hold higher levels and attract renewed capital, or if it will need another pullback before becoming attractive again.

$IGV all the key moving averages are clustered near current prices, and a pullback toward roughly $84.90 could serve as a healthy test to determine whether support holds. 

Importantly, despite negative sentiment in the media suggesting software is still weak, the pre-market action shows resilience. The sector is not being aggressively sold, which could signal that the worst of the selling pressure has already occurred.

When you think about software, you think $MSFT. It recently produced both Red Dog Reversal buy and sell signals, illustrating how traders can actively manage entries and exits within a volatile environment. 

A key level to watch for $MSFT is around $404, where the 8-day moving average sits. If Microsoft can hold that level, it may indicate that buyers are still supporting the sector and could open the door for software stocks to move higher intraday.

Beyond Microsoft, several major software names to monitor together as part of the sector’s overall health are: $CRM, $ORCL, $WDAY, $NOW.

$CRM in particular looks interesting. After a long downtrend, it may offer a “broken bounce” trade from lower levels, potentially providing a short-term rebound rather than a sustained leadership move.

So, software stocks are not being aggressively liquidated, despite broader market volatility and negative sentiment right now. 

Software could be a sentiment-driven contrarian opportunity you should watch closely.

If you enjoyed this video, please ❤️like and 🔁retweet

📺 THIS ISN’T “BLOOD IN THE STREETS” YET & AN OIL TRADE SETUP

The market is in a tactical, uncertain phase rather than a major selloff. 

$SPY recently broke below its 8/21-day moving averages, attempted to reclaim them, and was rejected, leaving the market in “no man’s land.”

In this type of environment, you should reduce risk and avoid aggressive swing trades, instead trading tactically around key levels.

A deeper pullback is still possible, including tests of a 5% correction (~$662 on $SPY) or eventually the 200-day moving average, which would still be normal market behavior rather than a crisis.

At the same time, the market has not declined enough to justify “blood in the streets” buying yet. $SPY is only about 3.5% off its highs, far from a meaningful correction, while $QQQ is even showing relative strength compared with $SPY and has not even broken last week’s lows.

In this Short video, I go through all levels below which I would begin allocating capital in my “blood in the streets” account.

In this Short video, I also cover an oil trade setup. A major driver of the market weakness is the sharp rise in oil prices, which are up roughly 12% amid geopolitical tensions.

You might consider fading oil strength, potentially by buying puts or selling premium on oil-related ETFs such as $USO.

So, with so many macro uncertainties, you should remain disciplined and tactical, focusing on key technical levels rather than reacting emotionally to headlines about crashes.

If you enjoyed this video, please ❤️like and 🔁retweet

The G7 to discuss a coordinated emergency oil release on Monday, with FT saying some officials see 300M to 400M barrels as the right range.

The release would be coordinated through the IEA, and FT says three G7 countries, including the U.S., already support the idea.

Lost 100 lbs through fasting. Completely changed my biomarkers.

Markets and health have the same lesson: the hardest part isn't the discipline.

It's trusting the process when everyone thinks you're crazy.

$DXY below 99. Down from 102 in three weeks.

Import costs rise. Purchasing power falls. The Fed can't fix both.

The $2 trillion private credit market is the next shoe to drop.

Nobody's watching.
